# sourcevision

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 107
- **Lines of code**: 21,152
- **Languages**: TypeScript (80), CSS (14), JSON (6), Other (3), JavaScript (2)
- **Git**: main @ 63d150a
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-03T04:46:21.594Z

## Architecture Zones

### Analysis Engine (`analysis-engine`)

Core analysis pipeline including file inventory, import graph extraction, Louvain community detection, route parsing, AI enrichment, and CLI orchestration.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: src/analyzers/components.ts, src/analyzers/imports.ts, src/analyzers/inventory.ts, src/analyzers/zones.ts, src/schema/data-files.ts

### Interactive Viewer (`interactive-viewer`)

Preact single-page application that visualizes analysis results through force-directed graphs, zone maps, route trees, and findings panels, consuming only schema types with zero analyzer dependencies.

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.96, Coupling: 0.04
- Entry points: src/schema/v1.ts

### Test Fixtures (`test-fixtures`)

Self-contained sample TypeScript project used as input data for integration and end-to-end tests, intentionally isolated with no imports from or exports to production code.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00

### Test Suite (`test-suite`)

Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests covering analyzer logic, schema validation, CLI workflows, and the serve command, with imports reaching into both analyzer and viewer zones.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54
- Entry points: src/schema/validate.ts

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/schema/index.ts` (20 imports)
- `src/schema/v1.ts` (14 imports)
- `src/viewer/types.ts` (13 imports)
- `src/analyzers/inventory.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/viewer/components/constants.ts` (8 imports)
- `src/util/sort.ts` (7 imports)
- `src/viewer/components/mini-charts.ts` (7 imports)
- `src/schema/data-files.ts` (6 imports)
- `src/analyzers/imports.ts` (5 imports)
- `src/cli/commands/constants.ts` (5 imports)

### External Packages

- `preact` (used by 23 files)
- `vitest` (used by 19 files)
- `typescript` (used by 3 files)
- `zod` (used by 2 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 1 files)
- `esbuild` (used by 1 files)

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/users/:id → tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- enrich.ts buries 4 of its 5 prompt templates inside a 200-line private function (enrichBatch, lines 392-597), interleaved with retry logic and config branching — prompt construction is untestable in isolation. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- enrich.ts duplicates the zone-ID deduplication loop verbatim at lines 834-842 and 889-898, and duplicates the validTypes/validSeverities arrays at lines 299-301 and 323-325 — four copy-paste blocks within a single file. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- enrich.ts line 741 uses a bare `catch {}` that silently swallows all exceptions from batch execution including potential programming errors, with no logging or error propagation. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- enrich.ts matches zones across passes by single-file overlap (`p.files.some(f => zone.files.includes(f))`) — this O(n*m) check can match the wrong zone if any file moves between zones across enrichment passes. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- enrich.ts meta-evaluation path (lines 638-705) has zero retry logic, unlike the batched path which retries 3 times — a single transient failure silently drops all severity re-evaluations for the entire pass. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- enrich.ts uses a type assertion (`as EnrichResult & { _updatedFindings?: Finding[] }`) at line 703 to smuggle an undeclared property through the return type, and zones.ts uses the same assertion at line 496 to consume it — this is a type-safety escape hatch that hides an interface gap. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- The schema/ barrel (index.ts) assigned to analysis-engine re-exports symbols from both the viewer zone (v1.ts) and the test-suite zone (validate.ts), creating hidden cross-zone dependencies for all 13+ consumer files — the schema layer should be its own zone or uniformly assigned. [critical]
- next-steps.ts is a new analyzer with zero test coverage at any level, and schema/data-files.ts is a new untracked schema module also without tests — two new production modules shipped with no verification. [warning]
- validate.ts is assigned to the test-suite zone but is imported at runtime by both the viewer (loader.ts) and the CLI (commands/validate.ts) — its zone assignment contradicts its actual usage as production infrastructure. [warning]
- The fileToZone map construction (useMemo with ZONE_COLORS[i % length]) is duplicated in views/files.ts, views/graph.ts, and components/detail-panel.ts — three copies of the same derived-data logic that should be a shared hook. [warning] (interactive-viewer)
- ... and 19 more

### Patterns

- next-steps.ts defines its own NextStep interface outside schema/v1.ts—this type is consumed by the MCP server but not validated or serialized alongside other schema artifacts, creating a shadow contract between the CLI and MCP layers. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- Schema files are consumed via two different import paths: analyzers use the barrel (schema/index.js) while the viewer imports v1.ts, validate.ts, and data-files.ts individually—this inconsistency is harmless but may cause confusion during refactoring. [info]
- The dual-build architecture (tsc for Node CLI, esbuild+Preact for viewer) means schema/validate.ts and its Zod dependency are bundled into the browser artifact—this increases the viewer bundle size for validation logic that could alternatively be stripped in production builds. [info]
- Viewer loader.ts validates all five data modules at load time using Zod schemas, providing a runtime schema firewall between analyzer-produced JSON and the rendering layer—this is a strong defensive boundary. [info] (interactive-viewer)
- Schema import paths diverge by zone: analysis-engine uses '../schema/index.js' (barrel), interactive-viewer uses '../../schema/v1.js' (direct). Both reach the same types — the barrel in schema/index.ts re-exports v1.ts. This creates two implicit conventions with no enforcement. [info]

### Relationships

- MCP server (mcp.ts) imports two analyzer functions (generateContext, deriveNextSteps) at runtime to synthesize responses on-the-fly—this is a live coupling point, not just a type dependency, making the MCP server sensitive to analyzer signature changes. [info] (analysis-engine)
- The NextStep interface in analyzers/next-steps.ts is the only cross-zone type not defined in schema/v1.ts—it escapes the schema contract and flows directly from analyzer to MCP server without validation or serialization. [warning]
- The viewer depends on three schema files (v1.ts for types, validate.ts for Zod schemas, data-files.ts for file constants) but has zero imports from src/analyzers/ or src/util/—the schema layer is the only coupling surface and acts as a clean anti-corruption layer. [info] (interactive-viewer)
- Unit tests import analyzer internals (louvain.ts, enrich.ts, route-detection.ts) that are not re-exported from analyzers/index.ts, creating a coupling to internal module boundaries that will break if analyzer files are reorganized. [warning] (test-suite)

### Observations

- High cohesion (0.94) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (analysis-engine)
- Bidirectional coupling: "analysis-engine" ↔ "interactive-viewer" (2+2 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "analysis-engine" ↔ "test-suite" (1+4 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "interactive-viewer" ↔ "test-suite" (1+1 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 46 files vs smallest with 5 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Contains 43% of project files (46/107) — may be too broad, consider splitting [warning] (interactive-viewer)
- High cohesion (0.96) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (interactive-viewer)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (test-fixtures)
- High coupling (0.54) — 4 imports target "analysis-engine" [warning] (test-suite)
- Schema bridge files (data-files.ts) and utilities (sort.ts, merge.ts) are correctly pulled into this zone by import gravity, keeping the analyzer-to-schema dependency unidirectional. [info] (analysis-engine)
- ... and 14 more

### Suggestions

- GENERIC_PREFIXES (inventory.ts:345) and GENERIC_SEGMENTS (zones.ts:29) diverge silently — zones includes 'pkg', inventory does not. Extract to a shared constant to prevent inconsistent path segment handling. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- Sort functions use inconsistent naming: sortInventory/sortImports (plain type name) vs sortZonesData/sortComponentsData ('Data' suffix). Standardize to one convention in src/util/sort.ts. [info] (analysis-engine)
- build.js and dev.js are the only plain JavaScript files in an otherwise all-TypeScript zone. Consider migrating to TypeScript for consistency and type safety of the build configuration. [info] (analysis-engine)
- commands/analyze.ts repeats the same 4-phase pattern (read JSON from disk → parse → call analyzer → write result). Extract a shared runPhase() utility to reduce the 313-line monolithic orchestrator. [info] (analysis-engine)
- enrich.ts at 925 lines mixes pass config, prompt building, CLI invocation, JSON parsing, finding extraction, and batch orchestration. Refactor: extract prompts.ts (buildMetaPrompt, pass configs), claude-cli.ts (tryCallClaude, timeout logic), parse-response.ts (tryParseJSON, extractFindings). [warning] (analysis-engine)
- mcp.ts has its own loadData() (line 32) while commands/analyze.ts reads files inline with readFileSync/JSON.parse. Neither shares a data-loading abstraction — changes to file format require updating both independently. [info] (analysis-engine)
- Create a shared src/viewer/utils/ directory for extracted viewer utilities (getZoneColor, buildFileToZoneMap, buildFlowDiagramData, meterClass, basename) and promote SV_DIR/TOOL_VERSION to a single src/constants.ts — would eliminate ~30 instances of duplicated code across both main zones. [info]
- Test infrastructure lacks shared utilities: temp directory management, fixture factories (makeFileEntry, makeInventory, etc.), and Claude CLI mocking are all defined inline. A shared tests/helpers/ module would reduce duplication and make test patterns consistent. [info]
- Dead code: setFileDetail function at detail-panel.ts:93-97 is defined but never invoked — its body is a no-op that extracts a filename then does nothing. [info] (interactive-viewer)
- Replace Record<string, unknown> detail panel contract with a discriminated union type (FileDetail | ZoneDetail | GenericDetail). Current duck-typing at detail-panel.ts:27-28 is fragile and silently falls through to generic rendering on misshapen data. [warning] (interactive-viewer)
- ... and 16 more
